studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period is 90 days for both arms. Primary and secondary outcomes are assessed at day 90.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: I = 135 (DiGA companion® shoulder digital health application) and C = 135 (standard of care physiotherapy); 1:1 randomization
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 (Intervention): Patients receive access to the digital health application (DiGA) companion® shoulder, which includes a 90-day personalized exercise therapy with evidence-based exercises and education, delivered digitally. The DiGA is a class I medical device (MDR). Arm 2 (Control): Patients receive standard of care for 90 days, including prescription of physiotherapy with usually 1 to 3 prescriptions of 6 units each, as provided in the therapeutic products catalog.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Shoulder-specific pain reduction measured with the Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS) after 90 days. Secondary outcomes: (1) Shoulder-specific functional improvement measured with the Shoulder Pain and Disability Index (SPADI) after 90 days; (2) Number of days of incapacity for work due to illness within the survey period measured via self-reporting at 1 measurement point (day 90). Inclusion criterion also references NPRS ≥4 for maximum pain intensity in the last 7 days at baseline.

audit.study_title: Multicenter randomized controlled trial (RCT) to demonstrate the positive care effects of the medical device companion® shoulder in adult patients with conservatively managed shoulder lesions with subacromial pain
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
